title: Abort: negative-refcount over-decref in `_PyFrame_ClearLocals` (`frame.c:101`)
description: Eval-loop exit_unwind clears a frame's operand stack via _PyFrame_ClearLocals; under OOM an opcode error path left a stale/already-dead _PyStackRef on the value stack, so PyStackRef_XCLOSE->Py_DECREF_MORTAL underflows the object's refcount. Confirmed as a negative-refcount abort on ft_debug_asan; on release the assert is compiled out (latent use-after-free, not pinned at this site by the minimal repro). Over-decref'd object is a MemoryError.
notes: Confirmed deterministically on ft_debug_asan (negative-refcount abort, top frames #11-#15: PyStackRef_XCLOSE@stackref.h:726 -> _PyFrame_ClearLocals@frame.c:101 -> _PyFrame_ClearExceptCode -> clear_thread_frame -> _PyEval_EvalFrameDefault@generated_cases.c.h:13908 exit_unwind). SCOPE CORRECTED (review 2026-06-19): the minimal repro reproduces ONLY on ft_debug_asan; ft_release/upstream = assert compiled out (latent UAF, NOT pinned at this site -- the repro exits clean / a different alloc fails) -> n/a; jit also does not hit this stackref path with the minimal repro (different OOM bug fails first) -> n/a. The OOM sweep trips several distinct latent bugs, so other vehicles are POSSIBLY-RELATED, not confirmed. In particular concurrent_futures_process on ft_debug_asan deterministically (8/8) hits a DIFFERENT list_dealloc heap-corruption segv (via unicode.split->PyList_New), NOT this assert -- the earlier 'cfp CONFIRMED in this group / 8 vehicles don't split / jit sibling assert / release segv = same underflow' claims were wrong (gdb-verified). Core bug is real and severe (eval-loop stackref over-decref / memory-safety), just scoped to the one solid signature. Minimization DONE (2026-06-19): deterministic 12-line stdlib repro `xml.dom.minidom.parse(0)` under the set_nomemory sweep range(100), 20/20 GIL=1 + 8/8 GIL=0 on ft_debug_asan. shrinkray on the xml_dom_minidom vehicle kept the fuzzer weird_classes setup (the final call's arg referenced it); substituting a trivial arg (0) freed it for removal -- the bare parse(0) sweep reproduces alone. The xml.dom.minidom path does NOT trip the sibling code_dealloc assert (Objects/codeobject.c:2440 _co_unique_id) the older multiprocessing.spawn/runpy reduction hit (that sibling is still a candidate for a separate id). dedupe.py = NEW.
